# TESS Program G08137

Title: Empirical Constraints On The Occurrence Rate Of True Hot Jupiter Progenitors

PI: Feinstein, Adina - Michigan State University

Type: SMALL

Summary: The formation mechanisms and evolution of short-period Jupiter-sized exoplanets is still unclear. Although we have discovered over three dozen planetary systems with ages < 500 Myr, follow-up campaigns have revealed that even the largest radius young planet planets (Rp ~ RJ) have masses consistent with Neptune. We propose to conduct a uniform search for hot Jupiter (HJ) progenitors orbiting young stars with ages younger than 1 Gyr. Using a combination of 2-minute and FFI observations, we will survey ~70,000 stars with kinematic memberships to nearby young moving groups, clusters, and associations. Our results will empirically constrain the occurrence rate of true HJs across stellar ages, tidal destruction timescales for HJs, and the influence of stellar flares on HJ atmospheric evolution.
